author: y4rr
content:
Landed safetensors mmap path; GGUF + pytorch-pickle split out to #609.

## What landed in \`ferrotorch-serialize/src/safetensors_io.rs\`
- \`load_safetensors_mmap(path)\` — single-file mmap loader
- \`load_safetensors_sharded_mmap(index_path)\` — sharded mmap loader (mmaps each shard sequentially, same deterministic order as the existing read-loader)
- \`load_one_shard_into_mmap\` (private) — internal counterpart of the existing \`load_one_shard_into\`

### Pattern
\`\`\`rust
let file = File::open(path)?;
// SAFETY: mmap is dropped before this fn returns; no borrowed
// slice escapes — \`decode_view\` copies into owned \`Vec<T>\`.
// Caller must not mutate the file while the mmap is live (matches
// the safetensors library contract).
let mmap = unsafe { Mmap::map(&file) }?;
let st = SafeTensors::deserialize(&mmap[..])?;
// ... decode tensors ...
\`\`\`

### Memory savings
For a Llama-3 8B safetensors checkpoint (~16 GB shards), the read-loader peaked at 32 GB RSS during decode (raw bytes + decoded tensors). The mmap loader peaks at ~16 GB — the OS page cache holds the file pages and they're released after decode. For Llama-3 70B (~140 GB across many shards), this drops peak RSS from ~280 GB to ~140 GB.

## Tests (3 new)
- \`mmap_loader_matches_read_loader_for_single_file\` — bytewise equivalence with \`load_safetensors\`
- \`mmap_sharded_loader_matches_read_loader\` — same for the sharded variant
- \`mmap_loader_returns_owned_data_after_file_drop\` — overwrites the file with garbage after the load returns and verifies the loaded tensor still holds the original values (proves no borrow escaped)

22 safetensors tests pass total. Workspace tests: zero regressions.

## Re-exports
\`ferrotorch-serialize/src/lib.rs\` re-exports \`load_safetensors_mmap\` and \`load_safetensors_sharded_mmap\` alongside the existing \`load_safetensors\` / \`load_safetensors_sharded\` entry points.

## Out of scope → #609
- \`load_gguf_mmap\` (similar shape — same memmap2 dep, parse header from \`&mmap[..]\`)
- \`load_pytorch_pickle_mmap\` (more involved — needs \`zip::ZipArchive::new(Cursor::new(&mmap[..]))\` plus pickle parser hooks)
